hero.title: 4 Days Saigon + Mui Ne Beach Resort.
hero.titleAccent: White Sand Dunes, Fairy Stream & 2 Nights Beachside.
hero.intro: Start in Mui Ne — the coastal resort town with the most dramatic sand dune landscape in Vietnam. Two nights beachside: the White Sand Dunes at dawn (04:45 departure for sunrise over the desert), ATV ride through the dunes, the red-clay Fairy Stream, and a fishing village still using the same traditional junk boats. Then Saigon: the War Remnants Museum, the Independence Palace, and the Nguyen Hue café street.
hero.heroImageAlt: Mui Ne white sand dunes sunrise Vietnam beach resort

quickFacts.saving: Save RM397 per person (20% online savings)
includes: Hotel accommodation (2 nights Mui Ne, 1 night Saigon)
All meals as specified (B, L, D)
White Sand Dunes sunrise tour with 4WD jeep

overview.heading: 4 Days Saigon + Mui Ne Beach Resort (2 Nights Mui Ne)
body: This four-day package gives Mui Ne what most Vietnam tours don't: two full nights, which means you actually experience the sand dune landscape at the right time — a 04:45 departure for the White Sand Dunes at dawn, when the dunes shift colour from pale grey to gold to orange before the heat of the day makes them uncomfortable. The ATV ride, the fishing village, the Fairy Stream walk, and the Red Sand Dunes at sunset on the same day fill Mui Ne completely.

Mui Ne is located on the south coast of Vietnam, approximately 3.5 hours northeast of Ho Chi Minh City, in Binh Thuan Province. The coastline gets more sun than almost anywhere else in Vietnam — over 300 days per year — because the Truong Son mountain range deflects most weather systems before they reach the coast. This makes Mui Ne one of the most reliably sunny beach destinations in Southeast Asia.

Saigon on day three is a manageable city tour after the beach days: the War Remnants Museum (the most visited museum in Vietnam, documenting the American War from the Vietnamese perspective), Independence Palace (the Reunification Palace where the Communist tanks famously crashed through the gates in April 1975), Notre Dame Cathedral, and the 19th-century General Post Office. The evening on Nguyen Hue Street — Ho Chi Minh City's main pedestrian boulevard — and the Café Apartment block at number 42 (a six-storey building where every apartment is a different café) offer the modern Saigon experience.

highlights.heading: Why This 4-Day Saigon Mui Ne Package Stands Apart

items.title: White Sand Dunes Sunrise — 04:45 Departure
items.body: The White Sand Dunes (Bau Trang) sit 25 kilometres north of Mui Ne town, in a landscape that looks unlike anything else in Vietnam — a genuine desert with white quartz sand dunes rising to 20–30 metres, flanked by small lakes. The pre-dawn departure (04:45) is timed so the jeep arrives before sunrise, when the dunes are cool underfoot and the light on the white sand shifts through lavender, pink, and gold. By 8am the dunes are too hot for comfortable walking. The ATV ride (2 persons per vehicle) covers the full extent of the dune field.
items.imageAlt: Mui Ne White Sand Dunes sunrise ATV ride Vietnam

items.title: Fairy Stream — Red Clay Canyon Walk
items.body: The Fairy Stream (Suoi Tien) is a shallow stream flowing through a narrow canyon of deep red and yellow laterite clay, with jungle overhanging the canyon walls. The walk is ankle-deep through the stream — bare feet, the water cool and clear. The canyon walls get progressively taller as you go deeper in, with carved-looking formations of red clay. The contrast between the red canyon, the emerald water, and the vivid green tropical plants above is completely distinctive and unlike any other landscape in Vietnam. A 30-minute walk in and back.
items.imageAlt: Fairy Stream red clay canyon Mui Ne Vietnam Suoi Tien

items.title: Mui Ne Fishing Village — Traditional Junk Harbour
items.body: Mui Ne's traditional fishing village is one of the most visually striking in Southern Vietnam: hundreds of traditional round woven bamboo coracles (thung chai) tied up along the shore, brightly painted junk boats at anchor, and colourful nets laid out to dry on the beach. The village wakes at sunrise and operates as a working fishing community rather than a tourist site — you walk through a real harbour at work. The catch comes in before dawn and is sold at the adjacent wet market by mid-morning.
items.imageAlt: Mui Ne traditional fishing village coracle boats Vietnam

items.title: Saigon City Tour — War Museum & Independence Palace
items.body: Ho Chi Minh City (Saigon) on day three covers the city's most significant historical sites: the War Remnants Museum (graphic documentation of the American War from the Vietnamese perspective — one of the most visited museums in Vietnam), Independence Palace (preserved exactly as it was in April 1975 when Communist tanks drove through the gate — the building where South Vietnam's leadership fell), Notre Dame Cathedral (1880 French Gothic), and the General Post Office (1891 colonial post office, still operating, with Ho Chi Minh's portrait replacing the colonial-era central clock). Evening: Nguyen Hue pedestrian boulevard and 42 Nguyen Hue Café Apartment Block.
items.imageAlt: Saigon Ho Chi Minh City War Museum Independence Palace Vietnam

itinerary.heading: Your 4-Day Saigon + Mui Ne Beach Itinerary

days.title: Arrive Saigon — Transfer to Mui Ne
days.description: Arrive at Tan Son Nhat International Airport (Ho Chi Minh City). Transfer to Mui Ne (225 km, approximately 3.5 hours by private vehicle, passing through Binh Thuan Province). Arrive Mui Ne midday. Hotel check-in and lunch. Afternoon: Red Sand Dunes (just 2 km from Mui Ne Beach — 30m high orange dunes against the sea, most dramatic at sunset). Dinner at local beach restaurant. Early rest recommended: 04:45 departure tomorrow morning.

days.title: White Sand Dunes Sunrise, ATV, Fairy Stream
days.description: 04:45: Jeep pickup for White Sand Dunes (25 km north, 30 minutes). Sunrise at the Bau Trang white quartz dune field — the best light in Vietnam for photography. ATV ride through the dune field (2 persons per vehicle, included). 07:30: Return to hotel for breakfast. 09:00: Fairy Stream walk — bare feet through ankle-deep clear stream through red and yellow clay canyon (30 minutes each way). Traditional fishing village — working bamboo coracle harbour, colourful junk fleet, wet market in full morning operation. Local lunch. Afternoon: Phan Thiet town (225-year-old market, Duc Thanh School where Ho Chi Minh taught 1910-1911, Pohsanu Cham Towers — the southernmost Cham Hindu ruins in Vietnam, 9th–15th century). 17:30: Red Sand Dunes sunset (the same dunes glow deep orange at sunset — a different experience from the white dunes at dawn). Seafood dinner at local restaurant.
suggestedActivities: 04:45 White Sand Dunes sunrise by jeep
Fairy Stream red clay canyon walk
Traditional fishing village and coracle harbour
Phan Thiet market and Duc Thanh School

days.title: Transfer to Saigon — City Tour
days.description: Morning checkout and drive to Ho Chi Minh City (225 km, approximately 3.5 hours). Arrive Saigon for city tour: War Remnants Museum (the most visited museum in Vietnam — photographs and exhibits documenting the American War from the Vietnamese perspective), Independence Palace (preserved as it was in April 1975 when Communist tanks drove through the gate), Notre Dame Cathedral (1880 French Gothic twin-towered church), General Post Office (1891 colonial building, still operating). Lunch at local restaurant. Hotel check-in. Afternoon/evening: Nguyen Hue Street pedestrian boulevard, 42 Nguyen Hue Café Apartment Block (six-storey residential building converted into multi-level café), Ben Thanh Market. Dinner at local restaurant.
suggestedActivities: Checkout and transfer to Saigon (3.5 hrs)

days.title: Saigon Free Morning — Depart
days.description: Free morning in Ho Chi Minh City for self-arranged activities. Options: Cho Lon (Saigon Chinatown with Thien Hau Pagoda), the Fine Arts Museum, a final coffee at a local Vietnamese drip-filter café. Transfer to Tan Son Nhat International Airport per agreed schedule.

whyBook.heading: Saigon + Mui Ne — Arranged by Malaysia's Trusted Agency Since 1988
whyBook.body: Mui Ne's White Sand Dunes are best experienced at dawn — which requires a 04:45 departure and precise timing. Dhesu has been arranging the Saigon-Mui Ne circuit for Malaysian travellers since 1988. Our local Mui Ne guides know the dune timing, the Fairy Stream access, and the best fishing village spots. We arrange the jeep, the ATV, and every transfer so the sunrise is never missed.

points.title: MATTA & IATA Registered
points.body: Fully licensed Malaysian travel agency. The Saigon–Mui Ne circuit is one of our most frequently booked South Vietnam packages.

points.title: 35+ Years of Vietnam Experience
points.body: Since 1988, we have been sending Malaysian travellers to South Vietnam. Our Mui Ne and Saigon guides understand exactly when and how to visit each site.

points.title: White Dunes Sunrise + ATV Included
points.body: The 04:45 White Sand Dunes jeep transfer and ATV ride (2 persons per vehicle) are included. No separate booking needed at the dunes.

points.title: Buy 3 Free 1 Promotion
points.body: Groups of 4 pay for 3 — the 4th person travels free. Ask about this offer when booking.

sections.heading: 4 Day Saigon Mui Ne Beach Tour from Malaysia
sections.body:
The 4-day Saigon and Mui Ne package is the most popular South Vietnam beach-city combination for Malaysian travellers. Mui Ne, located 225 km northeast of Ho Chi Minh City, offers the most dramatic sand dune landscape in Vietnam: the White Sand Dunes (Bau Trang) for sunrise, and the Red Sand Dunes for sunset, all within 25 km of the beach resort town.

Two nights in Mui Ne allows the full dune-and-coast experience — the White Dunes at dawn, the Fairy Stream's red clay canyon, the traditional fishing village, and the Red Dunes at sunset — all in a single memorable day. Saigon's War Remnants Museum and Independence Palace round out a South Vietnam experience that covers both the historical and the natural.

faq.heading: Questions About the 4-Day Saigon Mui Ne Package

items.question: Do Malaysians need a visa for Vietnam?
items.answer: No. Malaysian passport holders enter Vietnam visa-free for up to 30 days. Arrive at Tan Son Nhat International Airport with your Malaysian passport.

items.question: Why is the White Sand Dunes departure at 04:45 AM?
items.answer: The White Sand Dunes are most beautiful — and comfortable — at sunrise. The sand heats quickly once the sun rises, making late-morning or afternoon visits uncomfortably hot. The 04:45 departure reaches the dunes around 05:15–05:30, when the sand is still cool and the changing light (from lavender pre-dawn through gold and orange) gives the best photography conditions. The ATV ride and walk are also much more enjoyable in the morning cool.

items.question: Is the ATV ride included for all hotel tiers?
items.answer: Yes. The ATV ride (2 persons sharing one ATV vehicle) at the White Sand Dunes is included in the package for all hotel tiers. No separate payment is needed at the dunes.

items.question: What is the drive time from Saigon to Mui Ne?
items.answer: Approximately 3.5 hours (225 km) by private air-conditioned vehicle, passing through Binh Thuan Province. The route is highway most of the way. A comfort stop is included en route.
